description_long: This house features a boxy, two-story structure with a prominent, wide-hipped roof and a cylindrical rooftop element resembling a vent or cupola. The exterior is clad in light-colored panels with dark trim, and the windows are arranged in neat, symmetrical rows on both floors, each framed by bold, rectangular borders. The roof includes several skylights or solar panels, adding a modern touch to the otherwise traditional form. The main entryway is recessed and partially sheltered, with a small overhang and a single step leading up to the door. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, emphasizing function and geometric clarity. The house appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or composite panels, with metal and glass accents.
description: A two-story, boxy house with a wide-hipped roof, symmetrical windows, and a modern, utilitarian exterior featuring light panels and dark trim.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the house with two rows of windows and a partial view of the main entryway beneath the overhang.
description_view_1: The rear or side view highlights the broad roof, two skylights, and the symmetrical window arrangement without visible entry points.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the opposite side of the house, with a clear view of the entryway and a small step, as well as the cylindrical rooftop feature.
description_view_3: The back view displays the two skylights on the roof, the symmetrical window layout, and a recessed area that may serve as a secondary entrance or utility space.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed single door with small overhang and step, minimal decorative elements
